title: Pre-Registered Test of the κψ/Rψ Master Invariant in Quantum Computing Discourse (Domain 8)
author: Esposito, Giovanni
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This record contains the preregistered protocol for testing the κψ/Rψ Master Invariant, introduced in Appendix A (Gemini Law, ψ–GIE Field Guide), in a new out-of-sample domain: Quantum Computing Discourse (Domain 8). 
 The preregistration locks three quantitative predictions before data collection: – Resonance amplitude Rψ = 0.55 (expected 0.45–0.65) – Coupling constant κψ = 1.10 (expected 0.95–1.25) – Master Invariant κψ/Rψ = 2.0 (acceptance interval 1.8–2.2) 
 The falsification criterion is binding: if the 95% confidence interval for κψ/Rψ lies entirely outside [1.8, 2.2], the current form of the Master Invariant is rejected for Domain 8. 
 The deposit includes: – README.txt (human overview) – prereg_full.md (full protocol: background, sampling, RSI-Grok instrument, analysis plan, deviation policy) – manifest.json (machine-readable lockfile for predictions, instrument, and analysis plan) – voice_predictions.json (twelve-voice ψ–GIE bicameral predictions, treated as empirically testable operators, not decoration). 
 This preregistration is part of the ψ–GIE program and the Appendix A – Gemini Law (κψ) v25.6+ line of work, and is intended as the first out-of-sample test of the Master Invariant beyond the original seven domains (FX markets, stellar cores, JWST cosmology, S8 tension, Hubble tension, AI risk discourse, CRISPR ethics).

keywords: κψ, information-energy coupling, semantic entropy, quantum computing discourse, preregistration, Master Invariant, ψ–GIE, bicameral protocol, RSI-Grok
